name: Verb + -ing
summary: Many verbs use **-ing** when a second verb comes next. This lesson is only for verbs that **always** use **-ing** here. It is not for verbs that can also use **to + verb** with a different meaning.
contrast_note: If you need **to + verb** after the main verb, open **Verb + to + verb**. **Remember**, **stop**, and **forget** use **both** forms with different meanings — see **Tricky verbs (two forms)**. Some verbs use **both** with a **similar** meaning — see **Both forms (same idea)**.

name: Verb + to + verb
summary: Some verbs use **to + verb** when a second action follows. This lesson is only for verbs that **always** use **to + verb** here. It is not for verbs that can also use **-ing** with a different meaning.
contrast_note: For **verb + -ing** only, open **Verb + -ing**. When **-ing** and **to + verb** change the meaning, open **Tricky verbs (two forms)**. When both forms are OK with a **similar** idea, open **Both forms (same idea)**.

name: Tricky verbs (two forms)
structure: Same verb: **-ing** or **to + verb** — different meanings
summary: Some verbs allow **-ing** **or** **to + verb**. The meaning changes. This lesson groups those verbs so you can compare them.
contrast_note: **Remember doing** = you remember something you did before. **Remember to do** = do not forget something you must do later. **Stop doing** = you end an activity. **Stop to do** = you stop **so you can** do something else. **Forget doing** and **forget to do** work in a similar way (past vs future). This is **not** the same as **Both forms (same idea)** (for example **start** / **begin**).

name: Both forms (same idea)
summary: Some verbs allow **-ing** **or** **to + verb** after them. Often the meaning stays **similar**. This lesson is **not** for verbs where the meaning **clearly changes**.
contrast_note: If the meaning **changes** a lot, open **Tricky verbs (two forms)** (**remember**, **stop**, **forget**). For **only -ing** or **only to + verb**, open the other two main lessons.

name: Preposition + -ing
summary: Often the important part is a **small word** (**in**, **at**, **after**) or a **set phrase** (**interested in**, **good at**). After that you usually use **-ing**, not **to + verb**. This is not the same as **Verb + -ing** in the other lessons.
contrast_note: **Verb + -ing** is about the **main verb** (for example **enjoy playing**, **decide to go**). This lesson is different: you learn what comes **after** **in**, **at**, **after**, and similar words. It is almost always **-ing** (for example **interested in learning**, **after eating**). Try to keep the two ideas separate.
